title: 10 best herbal tinctures for neuritis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for neuritis are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate symptoms of neuritis, a condition characterized by inflammation or damage to nerve tissue. They help by reducing pain, inflammation, and promoting nerve regeneration. Effective herbal tinctures for neuritis include Ginkgo biloba, Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Avena sativa, Hypericum perforatum, Zingiber officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, Vitis vinifera, Cinchona officinalis, and Cannabis sativa. Without proper treatment, neuritis can lead to chronic pain, numbness, and weakness, significantly impacting daily life, causing frustration, mood changes, and sleep disturbances.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with neuritis because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in the maidenhair tree, particularly adenosine and caffeic acid, have been shown to effectively reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, alleviating symptoms of neuritis such as pain, numbness, and tingling. Additionally, the tincture's flavonoids may help to improve blood flow and nerve conduction, further contributing to its potential benefits in treating this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate neuritis symptoms.

Vasodilatory Properties: The vasodilatory effects of Ginkgo biloba tincture help to improve blood flow to the brain and peripheral nerves, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in cases of neuritis.

Neuroprotective Compounds: The neuroprotective compounds present in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to prevent or slow down the progression of neurological damage caused by neuritis, reducing pain, numbness, and tingling.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate neuritis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, thus protecting against neuronal damage and promoting healthy neural function.
Bilobalide: This triterpene derivative has neuroprotective properties, which help shield neurons from damage caused by neuritis, reducing symptoms such as pain, numbness, and weakness.

Ginkgolides: Specifically, ginkgolide B has been shown to inhibit platelet-activating factor (PAF), which can contribute to neuritis by causing inflammation and tissue damage. By blocking PAF, ginkgolide B may help reduce neuritis symptoms and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are primarily used to make tinctures for neuritis because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that improve blood flow to the brain and nerves.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to make tinctures for neuritis due to their high content of ginkgolides, which help to prevent platelet-activating factor and improve nerve function.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are also used to make tinctures for neuritis as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that enhance blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried leaves and vodka in the jar and seal it tightly to prevent air from entering.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks or up to 6 weeks for a stronger tincture.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Bottle the ginkgo biloba tincture in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with neuritis because it contains flavonoids and hyperforin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the nerves, alleviating symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and pain. Additionally, St John's Wort's sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress, which is often a contributing factor to neuritis. By addressing both the physical and emotional aspects of the condition, herbal tinctures of St John's Wort can provide natural relief from neuritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation in the nervous system, which helps alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as pain and swelling.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of St John's Wort tincture help protect nerves from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing the risk of neuritis.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: St John's Wort tincture contains compounds that have been shown to reduce pain perception and inflammation, making it a natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, reducing pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Naphthodianthrones (mainly hypericin): Exhibit potent neuroprotective properties, which help to prevent nerve damage and promote nerve regeneration.
Flavonoids (mainly rutin and quercetin): Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nerves, alleviating neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Hypericum perforatum are rich in flavonoids and xanthones, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in treating neuritis.

Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum contain a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which can help alleviate neuritis symptoms.

Roots: The roots of Hypericum perforatum are a good source of hypericin, a compound that has been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including neuritis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to facilitate solvent penetration during the maceration process.
Combine 250 grams of chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of 70 percent ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2 to 3 weeks with occasional shaking to ensure uniform extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with neuritis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's ability to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, numbness, and tingling associated with neuritis. Additionally, ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties may help support the body's natural response to stress, which is believed to contribute to the development of neuritis. By targeting the underlying causes of inflammation and oxidative stress, ashwagandha tinctures can provide relief from neuritis symptoms and promote overall nervous system health.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate Neuritis symptoms by calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that cause inflammation in nerve tissues, reducing pain and discomfort associated with Neuritis.

Neuroprotective properties: Ashwagandha has been shown to protect against neuronal damage and promote nerve regeneration, which can help alleviate symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and weakness caused by Neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: Withanolides, a group of alkaloids, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with neuritis, thereby promoting nerve health.
Withaferin A: Withaferin A, a steroidal lactone, has neuroprotective properties that help in preventing neuronal damage and promoting nerve regeneration, which can aid in the recovery from neuritis.

Ashwagandhans A and B: Ashwagandhans A and B, a pair of phenolic compounds, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that can help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby alleviating symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are used to make tinctures for neuritis because they are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera are used to make tinctures for neuritis because they contain alkaloids that have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects.

Seeds: The seeds of Withania somnifera are used to make tinctures for neuritis because they contain withanolides, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh or 100-150 grams of dried withania somnifera roots in the autumn season.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% ethanol in a ratio of 1:5 or 1:10 w/v to create a solution.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to extract the active compounds.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the tincture from the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively alleviate nerve pain and swelling. The curcumin present in turmeric tinctures reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, providing relief from symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect against free radical damage, promoting healthy nerve function and promoting overall recovery.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric contains a group of compounds called curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system associated with neuritis.

Cicloartisins: The tincture also contains cicloartisins, which have been shown to have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from pain and muscle spasms common in conditions like neuritis.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in turmeric tinctures have a sedative effect, helping to calm inflammation and reduce nervous activity that contributes to symptoms of neuritis, such as tingling and numbness.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, thereby alleviating symptoms of neuritis.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A bioactive compound that inhibits pro-inflammatory enzymes and has antioxidant properties, which may help protect against neural damage and promote recovery from neuritis.

Turmerone (α-Turmerone): A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis, promoting healing and recovery in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain high levels of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Roots: Roots are used because they also contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Barks are used because they contain a different combination of bioactive compounds that may complement the effects of curcumin in treating neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of dried curcuma longa rhizomes and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the curcuma longa into small pieces and soak them in 5 liters of 40% ethanol for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then filter it through a coffee filter.
Transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for 4 weeks.
Measure the final tincture and bottle it in 100ml dark glass bottles with a 1:5 ratio of curcuma longa to ethanol.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tinctures helps with neuritis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that target the inflamed nerves, reducing pain and discomfort. The tincture's unique blend of bioactive compounds, such as harpagoside and cimiragenin, has been shown to possess potent analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate the symptoms associated with neuritis, including numbness, tingling, and muscle weakness. By soothing the irritated nerves, devil's claw tinctures provide effective relief from the discomfort of neuritis, promoting overall well-being and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nerves, providing relief from neuritic pain.

Analgesic effects: The tannins and iridoid glycosides present in devil's claw have analgesic properties, helping to numb or reduce the sensation of pain, making it an effective remedy for alleviating neuritis symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Devil's claw also has antioxidant properties, which help protect nerves from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing inflammation that can contribute to neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Harpagide: Harpagide is a glycoside compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.

Procumbide: Procumbide is a sesquiterpene glycoside that has anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, alleviating neuritis symptoms.

Alkaloids (including Harpagoside): Alkaloids, particularly harpagoside, have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, providing relief from neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly harpagoside, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Barks: They are also used to make tinctures, possibly due to their similar composition to the roots and the potential for shared bioactive compounds.
Leaves: Although less commonly mentioned, leaves may be used due to their possible similar properties to the roots and barks, although more research is required to confirm this.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried harpagophytum procumbens root material in late fall or early spring.
Chop the dried root material into small pieces using a sharp knife or a mill.
Combine 100 grams of the chopped root material with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with neuritis because it has a natural ability to calm and soothe the nervous system. The active compounds in valerian root, such as isovaleric acid and valerenic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms that can contribute to nerve pain and numbness associated with neuritis. Additionally, valerian tinctures can promote a sense of calm and relaxation, which can help alleviate the anxiety and stress that often accompanies neuritis, allowing for a more peaceful and restful recovery.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian root contains compounds that calm the nervous system, reducing inflammation and pain associated with neuritis by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's polyphenols and flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help reduce swelling and discomfort in affected nerves and surrounding tissues.

Nerve protectant and regenerative effects: Valerian contains compounds that support the health of nerve tissue, promoting repair and regeneration of damaged nerves, which can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid is a sesquiterpene that may help with neuritis by reducing the excitability of neurons and modulating the GABA receptor, which can help alleviate nerve pain and inflammation.

Isovaleric acid amide: Isovaleric acid amide is a valerenic acid derivative that may help with neuritis by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help reduce neuronal excitability and anxiety-related symptoms associated with neuritis.

Acidic isovaleramide: Acidic isovaleramide is another sesquiterpene that may help with neuritis by acting as a sedative and anxiolytic agent, reducing nervous tension and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are primarily used to make tinctures for neuritis due to their high concentration of valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which have sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used in tinctures for neuritis as they contain valerenic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
Flowers: The flowers are used in tinctures for neuritis due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which has a sedative effect and can help alleviate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned valeriana officinalis roots in a warm place at 35 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 weeks.
Combine 250 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots with 750 milliliters of 80 proof ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place at room temperature for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with neuritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which alleviate pressure on the nerves and reduce inflammation. The tincture's active compounds, gingerols and shogaols, also possess analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to soothe nerve pain and prevent spasms. Additionally, ginger has antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve tissue, promoting healthy nerve function and alleviating symptoms of neuritis, such as numbness, tingling, and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's polyphenolic compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the nervous system, alleviating symptoms of neuritis.
Pain relief agents: Ginger's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in reducing pain associated with neuritis, providing relief from numbness, tingling, and shooting pains.
Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant compounds help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: A type of terpene, gingerol has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Shogaol: Another terpene found in ginger, shogaol has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that contribute to its pain-relieving and neuroprotective properties in neuritis.
6-Gingerol: A phenolic compound, 6-gingerol has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in neuritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: They are used for their similar properties to rhizomes, but may be more accessible or cost-effective in certain contexts.
Buds: They are used for their unique combination of active compounds, including volatile oils and phenolic acids, which contribute to their therapeutic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces approximately 1/4 inch in length using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped roots with 80-100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:5.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with neuritis because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids that work synergistically to soothe and calm inflamed nerves. The anti-inflammatory properties of maypop help reduce swelling and irritation in the nerve tissue, while its analgesic effects ease pain and discomfort. Additionally, maypop's ability to relax muscle spasms can help alleviate symptoms associated with neuritis, such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Maypop tincture helps to relax and calm nerve spasms, reducing discomfort and pain associated with neuritis.
Sedative property: The sedative properties of maypop tincture can help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are often exacerbating factors for neuritis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Maypop tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected nerves, providing relief from numbness, tingling, and pain associated with neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in neuritis.
Harmane: This indole alkaloid has a sedative effect and can help alleviate the anxiety and stress associated with neuritis, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
Isovitexin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage, contributing to the relief of neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used for their sedative and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Fruits: The fruits are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh or dried flowers of the plant at dawn or late evening when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Combine 1 part of the plant material with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar for a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every few days to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and label it with the date of preparation and the concentration of the extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with neuritis because of its ability to soothe and calm irritated nerves. The herb contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected nerve tissues. Additionally, lemon balm's mild sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress, common contributors to neuritis. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal lemon balm tinctures may provide relief from symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations associated with neuritis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Lemon balm tincture's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, which can contribute to neuritis symptoms such as pain and numbness.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: The anxiolytic and sedative compounds present in lemon balm tincture can help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that may exacerbate neuritis symptoms and promoting a sense of relaxation and relief.

Pain-Relieving and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon balm's essential oils, such as citral and nerol, have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.

Volatile Terpenes (e.g., Citral, Geranial, and Neral): A mixture of terpenes with potential anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties, which can help alleviate pain, spasms, and anxiety related to neuritis.
Apigenin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties, which can help reduce inflammation, oxidative stress, and neuronal damage associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of essential oils, including rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for neuritis.
Flowers: They are used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as pain and inflammation.
Roots: They are used due to their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of melissa officinalis flowers and leaves for the tincture preparation process.
Combine the melissa officinalis flowers and leaves in a clean glass jar with 50% vodka.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the melissa officinalis tincture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with neuritis because it has a unique ability to numb the pain and reduce inflammation in the affected nerves. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to increase blood flow to the affected area, which promotes healing and reduces discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation, allowing for improved nerve function and reduced neuritis symptoms. This natural remedy can provide quick and effective relief from the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Peppermint tincture helps to relieve pain and inflammation associated with neuritis, by reducing prostaglandins and other inflammatory compounds.

Antispasmodic Effects: Peppermint's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscle spasms and reduce nerve irritation, providing relief from the numbness, tingling, or burning sensations characteristic of neuritis.
Cooling and Sedative Properties: The cooling sensation of peppermint tincture can help to calm and soothe irritated nerves, reducing their sensitivity and promoting relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nerves, alleviating neuritis symptoms.
L-menthone: L-menthone has a neuroprotective effect, helping to protect nerve cells from damage and promote nerve regeneration, which can aid in the recovery from neuritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of menthol and menthone, which are known for their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: They are used for their ability to provide a sustained release of menthol and menthone, making them effective for managing neuritis symptoms over time.
Roots: They are used for their higher concentration of menthol and menthone compared to leaves, which can provide a more potent and effective remedy for neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 cup of chopped plant material with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow the mixture to steep.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tinctures helps with neuritis because it contains salicin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically as a tincture, it directly targets the affected nerves, reducing swelling and inflammation caused by irritation or damage. As a result, white willow tinctures alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as pain, numbness, and tingling sensations in the affected area. By addressing the root cause of nerve inflammation, white willow tinctures promote healing and restoration of healthy nerve function.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow tincture contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps to reduce inflammation and pain in the nerves, providing relief from neuritis.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The tannins present in white willow bark also contribute to its anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with neuritis.
Antioxidant activity: White willow's antioxidant compounds help protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve tissue, which can exacerbate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of white willow contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to relieve pain and reduce inflammation associated with neuritis.
Barks: White willow barks have been traditionally used to treat various neuralgias and arthritic conditions, including neuritis, due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry salix alba bark according to proper botanical protocols ensuring optimal quality.
Grind the dried salix alba bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine one part salix alba powder with three parts vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration allowing eight weeks preparation time.
Strain the liquid tincture from the solids through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the finished salix alba tincture in a cool dark place at room temperature for up to two years dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with neuritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate the painful symptoms associated with neuritis. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can also help to reduce anxiety and stress, common co-occurring conditions that can exacerbate nerve pain. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, chamomile tinctures provide a natural and gentle approach to managing neuritis-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce inflammation in the nervous system, which can alleviate symptoms of neuritis.

Sedative and relaxant effects: The flavonoids and terpenes present in chamomile tincture have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate neuritis symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds help protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis and other neurodegenerative conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them an effective treatment for neuritis.

Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain a high concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate nerve pain associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning when the dew has just evaporated.
Rinse the harvested flowers gently with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 250 grams of the rinsed matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days and shake it daily to ensure proper maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with neuritis because it contains bioactive compounds that target the root cause of inflammation and pain in the nerves. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on nerve endings, thereby relieving numbness, tingling, and shooting pains associated with neuritis. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidants promote healthy blood flow, which enables the body to regenerate damaged nerve tissue, promoting recovery and reducing inflammation. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal dandelion tinctures can provide natural relief from neuritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with neuritis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of dandelion tincture help to protect nerve cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis.

Choleretic effects: Dandelion tincture has been shown to increase bile production in the liver, which may help to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of neuritis such as numbness or tingling in the hands and feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are rich in bitter compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relieve neuralgia associated with neuritis.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe nerve pain and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and chop them into small pieces weighing 100g.
Mix the chopped roots with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar for infusion.
Store the jar in a cool dark place allowing the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids retaining the infused tincture.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles labeling each with date of preparation dosage and instructions for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet tinctures helps with neuritis because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the nerves. The tincture's flavonoids, particularly salicylic acid, have been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, which are hormones involved in inflammation, thereby reducing inflammation and discomfort in the affected area. Additionally, meadowsweet's anti-inflammatory effects may also help to promote blood flow and reduce pressure on irritated nerves, providing relief from neuritic symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.
remedy_properties: Salicylic acid content: Meadowsweet tincture contains salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with neuritis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in meadowsweet tincture help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to nerve inflammation and degeneration, making it a valuable adjunct for managing neuritis-related symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Meadowsweet contains various anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and polyphenols, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing in nerves affected by neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with neuritis.
Flowers: Contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly methyl salicylate, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried filipendula ulmaria root in the spring or summer for optimal potency.
Chop the root into small pieces and combine it with 100 proof vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks before use, allowing the flavors to meld and intensify.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive compounds in frankincense, such as boswellic acids, work to reduce swelling and pain in the nerves, providing relief from symptoms like numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. Additionally, frankincense's antioxidant properties help to protect nerve cells from damage caused by oxidative stress, promoting healthy neural function and reducing inflammation that can contribute to neuritis. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nerve pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin from your local pharmacy or online supplier.
Grind the resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Combine the ground resin with 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar, mixing well to ensure uniform coverage.
Allow the mixture to macerate at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, then strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter or fine-mesh sieve and transfer it to dark-glass bottles, labeling them with date and dosage instructions.

remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried oenothera biennis flowers and leaves, sourced from a reputable supplier.
Combine the dried botanical material with 150 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow maceration.
After the six-week maceration period, strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Discard the solids and transfer the tincture to small glass bottles with dropper caps, labeling each bottle with the date prepared.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated nerves and reduce swelling. remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Lavender tincture's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects can help alleviate neuritis symptoms by calming nervous tension and reducing stress, which is often a contributing factor to nerve inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in full bloom at dawn for optimal essential oil content.
Clean and dry the harvested lavender flowers by spreading them out on paper towels or a clean cloth for 24 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried lavender flowers with 600 milliliters of 40% vodka in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks.
Shake the jar daily for the first week and then weekly thereafter to facilitate extraction and mixing.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids after at least eight hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: California poppy tinctures helps with neuritis because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated nerve endings. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eschscholzia californica flowers at dawn for maximum potency and store them in a cool dark place.
Weigh out one part of the dried herb material or two parts of fresh herb material and combine it with 5 parts of high-proof vodka or brandy.
Pack the herb-vodka mixture into clean glass jars leaving about one inch of headspace and seal tightly.
Store the jars in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks to allow for gentle maceration and extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store it in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried hydrastis canadensis roots and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with 70% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the root mixture in the vodka for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure thorough infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label and store the finished hydrastis canadensis tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neuritis that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions such as sciatica, lumbago, and rheumatoid arthritis. Herbal remedies like St. John's Wort, Willow Bark, and Capsaicin have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective in soothing nerve inflammation and reducing discomfort associated with these conditions. These tinctures can be used externally or internally to provide relief from pain and stiffness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with neuritis is a blend of Ashwagandha, Passionflower, and St. John's Wort. Ashwagandha helps reduce inflammation and stress, while Passionflower calms nerve pain and anxiety. St. John's Wort has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in nerve repair. Taking 10-20 drops of each tincture, 2-3 times a day, can promote healing and alleviate symptoms of neuritis. However, consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have underlying medical conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with neuritis because of its unique ability to improve blood flow to the nervous system. This increased blood flow helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, ultimately alleviating symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and pain. Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used in some tinctures due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate neuritis symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 20-30 dried ginkgo biloba leaves and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container.

Clean the harvested plant material thoroughly with distilled water to remove any debris or contaminants.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least six weeks before use to allow the flavors to mature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tinctures helps with neuritis because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which work synergistically to reduce inflammation and pain associated with the condition. Chop the flowers into small pieces and combine them with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking it every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator and use a dropper to take 20-30 drops 2-3 times a day as needed.

Mix 50-100 milliliters of vodka 75 proof with the plant material in the glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow maceration.
Strain the liquid from the plant material through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowering tops of hypericum perforatum in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Weigh and combine 250 grams of the harvested plant material with 500 milliliters of 45% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking of the container.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it clearly with its contents and preparation date.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which alleviate pain and discomfort associated with the condition. remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of fresh zingiber officinale roots and wash them thoroughly with clean water for 5 minutes.
Dry the roots in a warm dry place for 2 weeks or use a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Combine the dried roots with 750ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar in a 1:3 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar every 2 days to aid infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place protected from direct sunlight for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part of dried flowers with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it vigorously for 2-3 weeks once a day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle after 2-3 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera tinctures helps with neuritis because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Vitis vinifera are used to make tinctures for neuritis due to their high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the vitis vinifera plant's fresh leaves and stems in the morning when they are at their highest moisture content.

Combine 250 grams of the dried plant material with 750 milliliters of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass container for 4 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate glass container discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa tinctures helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of dried cannabis sativa flowers and leaves from mature plants.
Steep the dried flowers and leaves in 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a dark glass jar.
